title: Roles and Groups for Bulk Management
description:
## Granting the Same Roles to Many Users

Your `acme` realm has 200 support agents who all need the same set of roles (`ticket-read`, `ticket-write`, `kb-edit`). New agents are onboarded weekly, and occasionally the role set changes for the whole team.

What is the cleanest way to manage this in Keycloak?

options.text: Create a **group** (e.g. `support-agents`), map the needed roles onto the group, and add each agent to the group.
Assign each of the three roles **individually to every user**, and repeat for each new hire.
Create a separate **client** per agent so each gets its own roles.
Put all the roles into a single **realm setting** that applies to everyone in the realm.

explanation:
**Groups** are collections of users that can carry **role mappings** and shared attributes. By mapping the roles onto a `support-agents` group, every member automatically inherits them, onboarding is just *add user to group*, and changing the team's roles is a single edit on the group. Option **b** works but is error-prone and doesn't scale — you must touch every user and risk drift. Option **c** misuses clients (which represent applications, not people). Option **d** doesn't exist as a feature — realm settings configure realm-wide policy, not blanket role grants to all users (and you rarely want *everyone* to have the same roles).

Note: Keycloak distinguishes **realm roles** (defined at the realm level) from **client roles** (scoped to a specific client). Groups can map either kind.

hints.title: Bundle once, reuse many times
hints.body: If you find yourself assigning the same roles to user after user, that's the signal to use a group with role mappings instead.
